waffles-with-greek-yogurt-and-berries.name: Waffles With Greek Yogurt And Berries
instructions: Spread 2 Tbsp. yogurt over each waffle and top with berries. Drizzle with honey and garnish with mint.

watermelon-cucumber-and-mint-salad.name: Watermelon, Cucumber, And Mint Salad

ingredients.item: Coarse salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Gently mix watermelon, cucumbers, and mint in a large bowl. Whisk olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper together in a small bowl. Drizzle over salad and gently toss to coat. Add feta cheese and gently mix.

wheat-berries-with-charred-onions-and-ka.name: Wheat Berries With Charred Onions And Kale

ingredients.prep: stemmed, leaves torn into 2-inch pieces (about 8 packed cups)

instructions: Combine wheat berries, 1 onion half, thyme sprigs, and 1 Tbsp. salt in a large saucepan; add water to cover by 2 inches. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium and simmer until wheat berries are just tender but still firm to the bite, about 35 minutes. Drain; discard onion and thyme. Place wheat berries in a large bowl; let cool.

Cut remaining 3 onion halves crosswise into 1/2-inch slices. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large cast-iron or other heavy skillet over medium-high heat; add onions. Season lightly with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onions are charred in spots, about 5 minutes. Transfer to bowl with wheat berries. Add 1 Tbsp. oil to same skillet. Working in 3 batches, add kale and cook, tossing occasionally, sprinkling with salt and pepper, and adding oil as needed between batches, until charred in spots, about 1 minute per batch. Add to bowl. Drizzle with lemon juice and any remaining oil; toss to coat. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

white-bean-and-tomatillo-soup.name: White Bean And Tomatillo Soup

ingredients.prep: top of bulbs trimmed off so tops of cloves are exposed

instructions: Preheat the oven to 260°C (260 degrees C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with aluminum foil.
Place tomatillos, poblano, and garlic head on the prepared baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for 30 minutes. Cool.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add andouille sausage; cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly crisp for 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer sausage to a plate.
Add onion, celery, and carrot to the Dutch oven; cook and stir for 5 minutes. Add cumin and chile powder; cook and stir for 5 to 7 minutes. Stir in cannellini beans; cook 3 to 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, remove and discard skins from tomatillos, poblano, and garlic. Transfer to the bowl of a food processor; pulse until combined.
Transfer liquified tomatillo mixture to the Dutch oven; stir in chicken broth and sausage. Cover the Dutch oven, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer for 25 minutes.
Divide soup among bowls; garnish with jalapeño slices and cilantro.

white-chocolate-frosting-argiro.name: White Chocolate Frosting
instructions: For white chocolate glaze first way
In the cutting wood with a knife, chop it.
Place it in a fireproof and wide bowl.
Cover the bowl with membrane and melt it on the microwave for 1′ to melt without losing its shape.
For white chocolate glaze second way
Alternatively, melt it into ben marie.
Heat in a pot with a thick base of cream.
Once it starts to swell (at 90 degrees C) we withdraw it from the fire.
Add the pieces in 3 doses, stirring around with a soft spatula.
Mix well after each dose.
You will see that once you have the first 2 doses of hot cream, the glaze begins to harden, but dropping the last dose of cream becomes smooth and smooth.
Make sure that when we mix the ingredients, we do not lock air into the mixture because bubbles will appear on the surface.
As long as it's warm, it's soft and fluid.
Add the sweet we want to dress on a grill that touches into a baking pan.
Sweet must be cold.
Slowly pour the cake with the glaze into its center, and it starts to run on the side, become hard and so covers and dresses all the sweet around.
After we prepare it and it is still fluid, we dress a shallow baking pan with membrane.
We're emptying the fluid lukewarm still.
Spread it all over the surface of the baking pan. Cover with membrane tangent to the entire surface. Let it cool completely.
Freeze the ganache in the refrigerator for 1 hour to stabilise.

When we need it, we empty it frozen as it is in the mixer's bucket. With the wire we beat it to flutter well and become like mousses. Then it swells, acquires more volume by hitting and flutters like mousses.
You can fill this mousse with pasta, cookies, cake, trunk.

white-chocolate-lemon-japanese-cheesecak.name: White Chocolate Lemon Japanese Cheesecake
instructions: In a bowl, beat together egg whites, sugar, 1 tbsp lemon juice, and cornstarch until stiff peaks form.
In a separate bowl, mix together cream cheese, sour cream, 1 tbsp lemon juice, lemon Jello, and flour. Combine thoroughly.
Combine the contents of both bowls and mix well. Add batter to a greased, springform pan and place in a water bath.
Bake at 177°C for 1 hr 15 min, then let cool for an hour. While cooling, make glaze by melting white chocolate in a bowl and mixing with lemon juice and milk until smooth.
Top cooled cake with glaze and refrigerate for 3 hours.
Serve.

white-gazpacho-teleculinaria.name: White Gazpacho
instructions: Peel the potatoes, cut them into pieces and throw them in a pot. Add the peeled water and garlic teeth, season with salt, light the heat and let it cook.
In the end, crush the soup with the magic wand, rectify the salt and set aside in the cold until serving time.
When serving, add the creams and some lemon juice. Serve well fresh, decorated with chopped chives.

white-stock.name: White Stock
instructions: Cut veal from bone.
Combine water, veal meat, veal bone, chicken, vegetables, and salt in a stock pot. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 5 hours.
Strain stock, and let cool. Discard vegetables and bones, use veal and chicken as desired.
